text_original: (It is the way of the Dao) to act without (thinking of) acting;
text_normalized: (It is the way of the Dao) to act without (thinking of) acting;

text_original: to conduct affairs without (feeling the) trouble of them;
text_normalized: to conduct affairs without (feeling the) trouble of them;

text_original: to taste without discerning any flavour;
text_normalized: to taste without discerning any flavour;

text_original: to consider what is small as great, and a few as many; and to recompense injury with kindness.
text_normalized: to consider what is small as great, and a few as many; and to recompense injury with kindness.

text_original: (The master of it) anticipates things that are difficult while they are easy, and does things that would become great while they are small.
text_normalized: (The master of it) anticipates things that are difficult while they are easy, and does things that would become great while they are small.

text_original: All difficult things in the world are sure to arise from a previous state in which they were easy, and all great things from one in which they were small.
text_normalized: All difficult things in the world are sure to arise from a previous state in which they were easy, and all great things from one in which they were small.

text_original: Therefore the sage, while he never does what is great, is able on that account to accomplish the greatest things.
text_normalized: Therefore the sage, while he never does what is great, is able on that account to accomplish the greatest things.

text_original: He who lightly promises is sure to keep but little faith;
text_normalized: He who lightly promises is sure to keep but little faith;

text_original: he who is continually thinking things easy is sure to find them difficult.
text_normalized: he who is continually thinking things easy is sure to find them difficult.

text_original: Therefore the sage sees difficulty even in what seems easy, and so never has any difficulties.
text_normalized: Therefore the sage sees difficulty even in what seems easy, and so never has any difficulties.
